=== THE LAW ===
ATTRIBUTED sentence -> MUST be ENTAILED by its source's verbatim span (not word-overlap: negation,
direction, units, modality, comparator, scope, and WHOSE finding all matter). OWNED -> reviewer's voice,
names no source, carries NO new particular (no number spelled or digit, no novel named entity). Fabrication
= an ATTRIBUTED sentence its source does not entail, OR an OWNED sentence carrying a particular.

=== WHY WE ARE HERE ===
Sol reviewed the reasoning engine and found SIX live fabrication paths. Our OWN adversarial suites all
passed -- including the release-boundary attack -- WHILE THE REAL VALIDATOR ADMITTED EVERY HOSTILE INPUT.
"The tests prove only that their chosen attacks were blocked." The released abstract ALREADY uses the
premise-free OWNED bypass. THE ARTIFACT IS BURNED.

THE BUILDER CANNOT VERIFY ITSELF, and NEITHER CAN AN OPUS ADVERSARY THAT SHARES THE BUILDER'S IMAGINATION.
The only accepted proof is: (a) CURRENT code FAILS a hostile test, then (b) after the fix, PASSES it, and
(c) Sol re-reviews and approves. Green canary is KNOWN WORTHLESS until it drives the real validator.


=== SOL'S SIX BURNS (read the full review at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BURN_V10.md FIRST) ===

1. ENTAILMENT IS WORD-OVERLAP (report_ast.py:434): digits + 25% bag-of-words. ADMITTED:
     SPAN "ratio ROSE by 1.5 points" -> SHIPPED "ratio FELL by 1.5 points".
     Also: "1.5 points"->"1.5 percent" (units unchecked); fabricated "9 percent" (single digits IGNORED
     at :449); fabricated year (year unconditionally exempt).
2. OWNED WITH NO PREMISES IS UNRESTRICTED (report_ast.py:611): Owned("The intervention causes fatal liver
     injury among children.") SHIPPED. The released abstract uses this (composer:387 -> report.md:5).
3. HEADINGS ASSERT ANYTHING (report_ast.py:540); publisher SKIPS them (publisher.py:186).
4. EVIDENCE TABLE PRINTS MODEL PROSE + unvalidated level/method (report_ast.py:624/:670).
5. CONNECTIVES MANUFACTURE RELATIONS (report_ast.py:548): "employment rose ...by contrast... output rose"
     -- the lie sits BETWEEN the clauses.
6. "MODEL NEVER TYPES A JOURNAL NAME" IS FALSE (report_ast.py:235): venues never added; "Science reports
     ..." SHIPPED under an AER card. Sub-4-char author names invisible.

Plus: OWNED VERDICTS CAN BE FALSE (facets are unproved strings, argument_planner.py:599; synthesis
contract does not validate the RELATION; the gate tries every operation and admits if any passes,
report_ast.py:605). COHESION creates false OWNED claims from templates (cohesion_pass.py:201). GENERALITY
is false -- the domain is HARDCODED (composer:212/:324/:382/:933/:827) and the compiled contract is never
passed in; a judicial opinion is refused at render (report_ast.py:356); zero evidence -> composer aborts
(composer:810) so it can never say "the literature does not settle this".

Sol's ladder (do them IN ORDER, do NOT score or stack until each rung clears its own safety checks):
  1. Add the hostile tests; CURRENT code must FAIL them.
  2. Replace lexical entailment.
  3. Close heading / owned-frame / table / connective lanes.
  4. Add proof-carrying verdicts (RelationProof).
  5. Replace cohesion templates; enforce dependency DAG.
  6. Run paired clinical/legal/thin fixtures at each rung.


YOUR TASK — SOL LADDER RUNG 1: write the hostile tests, and PROVE THE CURRENT CODE FAILS THEM.
This is the most important rung: it converts Sol's prose into executable proof that the holes are real.

Build /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/test_fabrication_paths.py with one test per burn, each driving THE REAL validator
(report_ast.validate_report / the real render / publisher path) — NOT a hand-built dict, NOT a reimplementation:

  T1 negation:      SPAN "ratio rose by 1.5 points"  CLAUSE "ratio fell by 1.5 points"  -> MUST be REJECTED
  T1b units:        "1.5 points" rendered as "1.5 percent"                                -> MUST be REJECTED
  T1c single-digit: fabricated "9 percent" not in span                                   -> MUST be REJECTED
  T1d fake year:    a fabricated number equal to work.year but not a real finding         -> MUST be REJECTED
  T2 owned-fact:    Owned("The intervention causes fatal liver injury among children.")   -> MUST be REJECTED
  T2b owned-number: Owned("The intervention doubled mortality among children.")           -> MUST be REJECTED
  T3 heading:       Heading(2,"Acemoglu proves that 47 percent of jobs will disappear.")  -> MUST be REJECTED
  T4 table:         a table row whose model-claim REVERSES its span, invented level/method-> MUST be REJECTED
  T5 connective:    two positive unrelated findings joined by "by contrast"               -> MUST be REJECTED
  T6 venue:         "Science reports ..." under an AER card                               -> MUST be REJECTED
  T7 owned-verdict: CONVERGES accepting BOTH "point in opposite directions" AND "not contradictory" -> at
                    most ONE may pass; a false reconciliation MUST be REJECTED

RUN IT NOW AGAINST HEAD. Report which tests the CURRENT code FAILS (i.e. the hostile input is wrongly
ADMITTED). Sol predicts ALL of them fail. If any test shows current code already blocking the attack,
your test is too weak — strengthen it until it exercises the real hole. Quote real output.
Do NOT fix the validator yet. Do NOT commit. This rung's deliverable is: the tests exist and current code
demonstrably fails them.

Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). 1	# Verdict: burn the artifact
2	
3	The reasoning engine is not fabrication-safe. The current release is burned.
4	
5	I reviewed revision `267e81bccd55d3b12b96d9eb2283e3c287098087`. The six requested files match HEAD; unrelated files in the worktree are conflicted, but I did not touch them.
6	
7	The existing adversarial suites all passed—including the release-boundary attack—while the real validator admitted every hostile input below. The tests prove only that their chosen attacks were blocked.
8	
9	## P0 fabrication paths
10	
11	### 1. “Entailment” does not test entailment
12	
13	[report_ast.py:434](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:434) checks only:
14	
15	- multi-digit number presence;
16	- 25% bag-of-words overlap.
17	
18	It does not check negation, direction, modality, causality, comparator, units, scope, or who made the finding.
19	
20	Exact admitted input:
21	
22	```text
23	SPAN: the local employment-to-population ratio rose by 1.5 points
24	      in regions that adopted the technology
25	
26	CLAUSE: the local employment-to-population ratio fell by 1.5 points
27	        in regions that adopted the technology
28	```
29	
30	The real renderer produced:
31	
32	> Writing in the American Economic Review in 2021, Bloom and Draca show that the local employment-to-population ratio fell by 1.5 points...
33	
34	That is an attributed sentence its source contradicts.
35	
36	Additional admitted attacks:
37	
38	- Fabricated single-digit `9 percent`: single-digit numbers are deliberately ignored at [report_ast.py:449](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:449).
39	- `1.5 points` changed to `1.5 percent`: units are not checked.
40	- Fabricated publication-year claim: a number equal to `work.year` is unconditionally exempt.
41	- Negative signs, ranges, fractions and uncertainty are not represented by the numeric checker.
42	
43	This alone invalidates the “182 fabrication attempts blocked” safety claim.
44	
45	### 2. OWNED with no premises is an unrestricted factual lane
46	
47	At [report_ast.py:611](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:611), an `Owned` node with no premises passes after checking only digits and known author names.
48	
49	Exact admitted input:
50	
51	```python
52	Owned(text="The intervention causes fatal liver injury among children.")
53	```
54	
55	It rendered unchanged.
56	
57	So did:
58	
59	```python
60	Owned(text="The intervention doubled mortality among children.")
61	```
62	
63	Spelled quantities are allowed in this lane. Novel lowercase entities—`mortality`, `children`—are never checked.
64	
65	The released abstract already uses this bypass. [cellcog_composer.py:387](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:387) emits a premise-free factual claim that AI is a general-purpose technology of the Fourth Industrial Revolution restructuring work across industries. It appears at [report.md:5](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/release/report.md:5).
66	
67	That is why the current artifact is burned, not merely “at risk.”
68	
69	### 3. Headings are untyped, unreceipted prose
70	
71	[report_ast.py:540](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:540) validates a heading only for non-emptiness.
72	
73	Exact admitted input:
74	
75	```python
76	Heading(2, "Acemoglu proves that 47 percent of jobs will disappear.")
77	```
78	
79	It rendered with an empty sidecar:
80	
81	```markdown
82	## Acemoglu proves that 47 percent of jobs will disappear.
83	```
84	
85	The publisher explicitly skips headings during receipt completeness checking at [publisher.py:186](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/publisher.py:186).
86	
87	The AST therefore has a third voice: `Heading`, which can assert anything.
88	
89	### 4. The evidence table prints model-written prose and unverified fields
90	
91	[report_ast.py:624](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:624) reads the model-authored `claim`. [report_ast.py:670](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:670) prints it. It also prints `level` and `method` without validating either against the span.
92	
93	The real validator accepted and rendered this table:
94	
95	| Actual span | Shipped model claim/fields |
96	|---|---|
97	| ratio **rose** 1.5 points | ratio **fell** 1.5 points; level=`children with cancer`; method=`randomized trial` |
98	| assistance **raised** output 40% | assistance **lowered** output 40%; level=`pregnant patients`; method=`meta-analysis` |
99	| exposure **reached** 32% | exposure **fell to** 32%; level=`criminal defendants`; method=`binding precedent` |
100	
101	All three rows passed.
102	
103	Table lines are skipped by the publisher’s prose-receipt check, while the sidecar records artificial strings such as `TABLE_ROW::<card-id>`, not the cells the judge reads. This reopens the exact “fabrication-proof table prints model prose” defect.
104	
105	### 5. Cross-source connectives manufacture relations
106	
107	At [report_ast.py:548](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:548), the model may choose `while`, `whereas`, `by contrast`, `but`, or `yet`. Each clause is checked separately; nobody proves the relation introduced by the connective.
108	
109	Two positive, unrelated findings were admitted as:
110	
111	> ...employment rose..., **by contrast** ...task output rose...
112	
113	Neither source entails the contrast. The lie sits between the clauses, outside both span checks.
114	
115	### 6. “The model never types a journal name” is false
116	
117	`names_a_source()` claims to know surnames and venues, but [report_ast.py:235](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:235) populates only author strings. Venues are never added.
118	
119	This passed:
120	
121	```text
122	Science reports that the local employment-to-population ratio rose...
123	```
124	
125	under an American Economic Review card.
126	
127	Short author names under four characters are also deliberately invisible. If authors are stored as full names, a surname alone can likewise evade an exact full-string match.
128	
129	## Can an owned verdict be false?
130	
131	Yes. Determinism only proves reproducibility.
132	
133	Three independent defects make false verdicts admissible:
134	
135	1. Declared facets are unproved strings.  
136	   [argument_planner.py:599](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py:599) trusts `level`, `method`, and `horizon` directly from the card. There is no span binding for those tags.
137	
138	2. The synthesis contract does not validate the asserted relationship.  
139	   Most operations have no operation-specific semantic check. `CONVERGES`, `CONTRASTS_DIRECTION`, `ESTABLISHES`, `DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH`, and `REMAINS_UNRESOLVED` can pass on anchoring alone.
140	
141	   I passed the same premises through `CONVERGES` with both:
142	
143	   ```text
144	   These findings point in opposite directions...
145	   These findings are not contradictory...
146	   ```
147	
148	   Both were accepted.
149	
150	3. The shipping gate discards the planned operation.  
151	   [report_ast.py:605](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:605) tries every operation and admits the sentence if any one passes. `Owned` does not even carry an `operation` field.
152	
153	The planner templates also make invalid logical leaps. Different units license “not directly comparable”; they do not, by themselves, prove “not contradictory,” “what holds at A does not establish B,” or that observed differences are explained by level.
154	
155	### A defensible verdict
156	
157	A verdict needs a proof object, not tags:
158	
159	```text
160	RelationProof
161	  operation
162	  premise claim-atom IDs
163	  verified shared dimensions
164	  verified differing dimensions
165	  polarity/modality/comparator for each premise
166	  rule whose preconditions were satisfied
167	  exact facet-supporting spans
168	  rendered conclusion template
169	```
170	
171	`SAME_OUTCOME_DIFFERENT_UNIT` should license only:
172	
173	> These findings concern different units and are not directly comparable.
174	
175	“Not contradictory” requires substantially more: the same construct, compatible population/time/comparator, opposed surface results, and a formal demonstration that both can simultaneously be true because their scopes differ. If any facet is unproved, no verdict ships.
176	
177	## Is cohesion safe with real LLM prose?
178	
179	No.
180	
181	It cannot mutate an `Attributed` object; that narrow claim is true. But it can create false `Owned` claims, and the owned-frame gate accepts them.
182	
183	With two truthful admitted paragraphs whose only differing declared field was industry, the real pass generated and admitted:
184	
185	> The same question can be put to healthcare, where the constraints on adoption differ from those in manufacturing.
186	
187	Neither span mentioned constraints, adoption differences, or that comparison. The claim comes from the template at [cohesion_pass.py:201](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cohesion_pass.py:201).
188	
189	Other unsafe templates assert that:
190	
191	- two levels “do not answer the same question”;
192	- a level shift is “not merely one of scale”;
193	- differing methods agree and therefore carry more weight;
194	- mechanisms may change over time;
195	- sectoral constraints differ;
196	- the answer differs across sectors.
197	
198	The pass itself makes no LLM call. Its risk when fed real LLM prose is therefore already present deterministically.
199	
200	Reordering is also dependency-blind. `_assert_frozen()` proves attributed-node identity, but not that:
201	
202	- verdicts remain after every premise they adjudicate;
203	- anaphora still has the same antecedent;
204	- paragraph order preserves argumentative dependencies.
205	
206	A safe cohesion pass may choose only among proof-carrying transitions such as:
207	
208	> The next evidence concerns the firm level rather than the occupation level.
209	
210	That transition must carry the adjacent card IDs and verified facet bindings. Reordering must preserve an explicit dependency DAG.
211	
212	## Generality claim: false
213	
214	There is no `if domain ==` branch because the entire domain is hardcoded.
215	
216	Examples:
217	
218	- Labor-specific writer prompt: [cellcog_composer.py:212](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:212)
219	- Labor-specific outline: [cellcog_composer.py:324](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:324)
220	- Labor-specific abstract: [cellcog_composer.py:382](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:382)
221	- Labor-specific title: [cellcog_composer.py:933](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:933)
222	- Composer always calls the task-72 default contract: [cellcog_composer.py:827](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:827)
223	
224	The compiled research contract is not passed into this composer.
225	
226	### Legal attack
227	
228	A valid bound judicial opinion passed `OFFICIAL_TEXT` policy in the provenance graph, then `CardBundle.resolve()` refused it:
229	
230	```text
231	EXPRESSION_KIND_IS_NOT_RENDERABLE_AS_A_CITATION: 'official_text'
232	```
233	
234	[report_ast.py:356](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:356) renders only journal and proceedings expressions. Therefore primary cases and statutes cannot reach the page.
235	
236	The alleged “5/5 doctrinal findings reach the page” result measures only ledger assignment. My five-card doctrinal fixture assigned all five findings, but:
237	
238	- four were assigned under “How the Fourth Industrial Revolution differs...”;
239	- one under occupational displacement;
240	- 19 of 26 subsections were empty;
241	- none could resolve as an official legal citation.
242	
243	### Thin-evidence attack
244	
245	With zero admitted cards, [cellcog_composer.py:810](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:810) aborts.
246	
247	The ledger path is hashed but never read for coverage conclusions. Consequently a saturated search returning no admissible evidence cannot produce the correct answer, “the evidence does not settle this.” Zero evidence always means no report.
248	
249	### Clinical behavior
250	
251	The same composer would:
252	
253	- use the AI/labor outline and title;
254	- demand a figure in every attributed finding;
255	- lack trial-specific population/intervention/comparator/outcome/time proof;
256	- treat guideline or registry official text as unrenderable;
257	- permit dose/unit/polarity swaps through lexical overlap.
258	
259	## General fix architecture
260	
261	| Fix | Clinical | Legal/comparative | Thin evidence | Domain change must be data |
262	|---|---|---|---|---|
263	| Proof-carrying claim atoms | PICO, endpoint, time, design, estimate, uncertainty and units each bind to exact spans | jurisdiction, authority, issue, holding/rule, posture and validity bind to exact text | Missing atoms produce UNKNOWN, never guessed tags | Add evidence-act and facet-schema rows; no topic regex |
264	| Close every prose lane | Trial headings/tables use validated atoms | Case/statute citations get official-text render templates | Coverage headings state only audited search status | Add citation/render templates and source-kind rows |
265	| Typed relation proofs | Pool/contrast only compatible endpoints and populations | Compare holdings only on the same legal issue, accounting for hierarchy and jurisdiction | One source or incompatible evidence yields `UNRESOLVED` | Add allowed relation rules and preconditions |
266	| Coverage conclusion node | “No eligible trial evidence found” only after registered searches complete | Same for courts/statutes/jurisdictions actually searched | Zero evidence can correctly ship a bounded non-settlement conclusion | Add required route families and saturation rules |
267	| Dependency-safe cohesion | Transitions state only verified design/population deltas | Transitions state only verified jurisdiction/authority deltas | No invented movement when evidence is absent | Add facet display names; code remains domain-neutral |
268	
269	Code should implement the universal IR, proof checking, dependency preservation, and fail-closed rendering. A new domain should normally require rows in registries—evidence acts, facets, source kinds, citation templates, relation rules, and routing requirements—not edits to Python logic. A code edit is justified only for a genuinely new universal logical primitive, not a new topic.
270	
271	## Additional burns
272	
273	- The planner’s second-hand-source detector is not on the body-selection path. The ledger licenses every admitted card, including cards the planner marked fatal.
274	- `pair_ok()` identifies independent works by DOI inequality. Legal sources often have no DOI, while duplicated versions can have differing identifiers.
275	- `fact_use_ledger.natural_role()` trusts model-derived `has_number` at [fact_use_ledger.py:316](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/fact_use_ledger.py:316).
276	- Narrate-once is exact only over the ledger’s derived finding IDs, not cards or necessarily works. Its fallback work identity can collide for anonymous sources sharing year and venue.
277	- Sparse corpora have no subsection floor. The five-card legal measurement confirmed 19 empty subsections.
278	- The release verifier rechecks byte bindings and attribution targets, but not semantic entailment. A perfectly hashed contradiction remains a contradiction.
279	
280	Recommended ladder on frozen corpora:
281	
282	1. Add the hostile tests above; current code must fail them.
283	2. Replace lexical entailment.
284	3. Close heading/frame/table/connective lanes.
285	4. Add proof-carrying verdicts.
286	5. Replace cohesion templates and enforce dependencies.
287	6. Run paired clinical, legal and thin fixtures at each rung.
288	
289	Do not score or stack these changes until each rung independently clears its criterion-level safety checks.

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE TYPED REPORT AST — THE LAW, MECHANISED. There is no other way to say a sentence.
3	
4	    Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN)
5	    or OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries no new particular, MAY be non-entailed).
6	    THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. The model-written `claim` is a display cache; nothing is
7	    ever validated against it.
8	
9	WHY THIS FILE EXISTS, AND WHY IT IS NOT ANOTHER PREFLIGHT
10	---------------------------------------------------------
11	Six adversary attacks succeeded while `test_gate_is_wired.py` was 16/16 GREEN and nothing had been
12	weakened. The reason was not a weak check. It was that **the checks certified a lane the fabrication no
13	longer used**: 6,463 lines of correct, self-tested modules were dead code, and the composer imported not
14	one of them. `provenance.py` passed its own 18/18 self-test while the P0 it was built to stop sat live on
15	disk.
16	
17	So the gate is no longer a function the writer is asked to call. IT IS THE ONLY TYPE A SENTENCE CAN HAVE.
18	A sentence that has not been through here is not "ungated" — IT DOES NOT EXIST, because prose is never
19	concatenated anywhere in this pipeline. `render()` is the only thing that emits characters, it emits them
20	only from validated nodes, and `publisher.py` is the only thing that may write them into the release
21	directory.
22	
23	WHAT CHANGED IN THE SHAPE OF A SENTENCE
24	---------------------------------------
25	The old composer inferred WHO A SENTENCE WAS ABOUT BY LOOKING FOR SURNAMES IN IT (`_cited_cards`). That
26	is how "Bresnahan reports task displacement" — a real mechanism, a real paper, a FABRICATED BINDING —
27	was gated against whichever card the regex happened to hit first. Sol: **DO NOT INFER VOICE OR SOURCE
28	IDENTITY FROM SURNAMES.**
29	
30	Here, the model does not write attribution prose AT ALL, and it does not get to imply a source by naming
31	one. It emits a bare FINDING plus THE CARD ID it came from:
32	
33	    Attributed(clauses=[Clause(card_id='...:76927-77299', text='the employment-to-population ratio
34	                               falls by 0.37 percentage points per robot per thousand workers')])
35	
36	and THIS FILE renders the attribution — from the graph, from the expression the SOURCE POLICY selected.
37	The model cannot copy an attribution, cannot invent one, and cannot name a journal whose bytes we do not
38	hold, because it never types the journal's name.
39	
40	A CROSS-SOURCE SENTENCE IS A CONJUNCTION OF ATTRIBUTED CLAUSES, EACH BOUND TO ITS OWN CARD. That keeps
41	the heaviest criterion on the board (critical synthesis, w=0.0800) reachable — the old gate deleted every
42	comparative sentence — while the fabricated binding still dies, because the clause that says "task
43	displacement" names Bresnahan's card_id and 'task displacement' is not in Bresnahan's span.
44	
45	THE CHAIN, WHICH IS THE WHOLE POINT
46	-----------------------------------
47	    sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression -> attribution
48	
49	`CardBundle.resolve()` IS that chain, as one function, and it is the only door. Nothing in this file, in
50	`cellcog_composer.py`, or in `publisher.py` reads a card's `attribution`, `venue`, `doi` or `authors` to
51	decide anything. Those are display caches. The graph decides.
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import hashlib
56	import re
57	import sys
58	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
59	from pathlib import Path
60	
61	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
62	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
63	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
64	
65	import provenance as P  # noqa: E402
66	from synthesis_contract import Premise, Synthesis, validate, OPERATIONS  # noqa: E402
67	
68	
69	# =================================================================================================
70	# 0. THE NODES. A report is a list of these and NOTHING ELSE.
71	# =================================================================================================
72	
73	@dataclass(frozen=True)
74	class Clause:
75	    """ONE finding, from ONE card. The text is the FINDING ONLY — no attribution, no journal, no year.
76	
77	    `card_id` is not a hint and it is not decoration: it is the sentence's ONLY connection to reality,
78	    and it is checked against the bytes. The model may not name a source in `text`; if it does, the
79	    clause is refused (SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT), because a named source in model prose is a source
80	    WE DID NOT RESOLVE.
81	    """
82	    card_id: str
83	    text: str
84	
85	
86	#: How clause i>0 joins the clause before it. A CLOSED SET — the model may not write connective prose,
87	#: because a connective is where an unsourced particular hides ("..., which explains why 40% of...").
88	CONNECTIVES = ('while', 'whereas', 'by contrast', 'and', 'though', 'but', 'yet')
89	
90	
91	@dataclass(frozen=True)
92	class Attributed:
93	    """A sentence that names sources. EVERY clause is entailed by ITS OWN card's verbatim span."""
94	    clauses: tuple[Clause, ...]
95	    connective: str = 'while'
96	
97	    def card_ids(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
98	        return tuple(c.card_id for c in self.clauses)
99	
100	
101	@dataclass(frozen=True)
102	class Owned:
103	    """The reviewer's voice. MAY BE NON-ENTAILED — that is what insight IS.
104	
105	    It must name NO source and carry NO new particular. Two regimes, one type:
106	
107	      * `premise_ids` non-empty  -> a SYNTHESIS over admitted premises. It must additionally pass a
108	        typed operation in `synthesis_contract` (a mechanism may appear ONLY if a premise states it).
109	        This is the body lane.
110	      * `premise_ids` empty      -> a FRAME sentence (an abstract's "Objective.", a section's bridge).
111	        It is licensed by nothing and may therefore assert nothing particular: no number, no source, no
112	        proper noun the review has not already earned. This is the lane the hand-written abstract used
113	        to bypass the law through, and it is now a TYPE with a CHECK, not an f-string in write_report().
114	    """
115	    text: str
116	    premise_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
117	
118	
119	@dataclass(frozen=True)
120	class Heading:
121	    level: int
122	    text: str
123	
124	
125	@dataclass(frozen=True)
126	class ParagraphBreak:
127	    """A paragraph boundary, AS A NODE — so a paragraph cannot be made by concatenating a '\\n\\n'.
128	
129	    If whitespace were prose, the writer would have a lane that emits characters without a type, and
130	    every hole in this file's header began as a lane that emitted characters without a type.
131	    """
132	
133	
134	@dataclass(frozen=True)
135	class EvidenceTable:
136	    """Every cell is a field of a card whose binding RE-VERIFIES at render time. No model prose."""
137	    card_ids: tuple[str, ...]
138	    caption: str = ('**Table 1. Quantitative findings across the reviewed literature.** Each row reports '
139	                    'a figure stated by the cited paper itself, verified against that paper\'s own bytes.')
140	
141	
142	Node = Attributed | Owned | Heading | ParagraphBreak | EvidenceTable
143	
144	
145	# =================================================================================================
146	# 1. THE CHAIN.  sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation + hash -> permitted expression
147	# =================================================================================================
148	
149	@dataclass(frozen=True)
150	class Resolution:
151	    ok: bool
152	    card_id: str
153	    refusal: str = ''
154	    manifestation_id: str = ''
155	    content_hash: str = ''
156	    expression_id: str = ''            # the expression whose bytes the span ACTUALLY came from
157	    names_expression_id: str = ''      # the expression the sentence is PERMITTED to name
158	    span: str = ''                     # THE VERBATIM SPAN. THE ONLY EVIDENCE.
159	    span_start: int = -1
160	    span_end: int = -1
161	    work_id: str = ''
162	    attribution: str = ''              # RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH. Never copied off the card.
163	    card: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
164	
165	
166	def _binding_from_card(c: dict) -> dict:
167	    """The binding a card carries, in the shape `Graph.verify_span()` demands.
168	
169	    A card that has not been through `graph.bind_span()` HAS NO BINDING, and this returns {} rather than
170	    a plausible-looking dict assembled from a DOI and two integers. That distinction is the P0: a DOI
171	    names a WORK, and a work has no bytes.
172	    """
173	    if not c.get('manifestation_id') or not c.get('content_hash'):
174	        return {}
175	    for k in ('span_start', 'span_end', 'span_raw'):
176	        if c.get(k) is None:
177	            return {}
178	    return dict(
179	        manifestation_id=c['manifestation_id'],
180	        content_hash=c['content_hash'],
181	        span_start=c['span_start'],
182	        span_end=c['span_end'],
183	        text=c['span_raw'],
184	        permitted_expression_ids=list(c.get('permitted_expression_ids') or []),
185	    )
186	
187	
188	class CardBundle:
189	    """ONE card lane. ONE graph. ONE policy. THE SEAM IS GONE.
190	
191	    There were TWO DISCONNECTED CARD LANES: the miner wrote `evidence_cards_v2.json` and the composer
192	    read `evidence_cards.json` — a file whose cards carry no manifestation, no hash and no expression,
193	    and whose four Frey & Osborne cards were mined out of an ORA LANDING PAGE. The composer could not
194	    have detected that, because the lane it read had nothing in it to detect with.
195	
196	    A bundle is constructed from an EXPLICIT path plus the graph, and it REFUSES to exist if the bytes
197	    on disk are not the bytes it was promised.
198	    """
199	
200	    def __init__(self, cards: list[dict], graph: P.Graph,
201	                 policy: P.SourcePolicy = P.JOURNAL_ONLY,
202	                 cards_sha: str = '', graph_sha: str = '', ledger_sha: str = ''):
203	        self.graph = graph
204	        self.policy = policy
205	        self.cards_sha = cards_sha
206	        self.graph_sha = graph_sha
207	        self.ledger_sha = ledger_sha
208	        # A DUPLICATE CARD ID IS A REFUSAL, NOT AN OVERWRITE.
209	        #
210	        # This dict used to be filled with `self.cards[cid] = c`, and the miner's id is
211	        # `{doi}:{span_start}-{span_end}` — so TWO FINDINGS MINED FROM ONE SPAN COLLIDE. On the first
212	        # real bundle, 13 of 52 cards were silently overwritten: the composer printed "52 cards, 0
213	        # refused" and had 39. The evidence did not fail a check. IT LEFT WITHOUT ONE, which is the
214	        # quieter half of every defect in this file's header. And a sentence citing a colliding id
215	        # names an AMBIGUOUS card: same span, different claim, different act, different table row.
216	        self.cards: dict[str, dict] = {}
217	        dupes: dict[str, int] = {}
218	        for c in cards:
219	            cid = c.get('id')
220	            if not cid:
221	                raise ValueError('a card with no `id` cannot be cited by a sentence, and is REFUSED')
222	            if cid in self.cards:
223	                dupes[cid] = dupes.get(cid, 1) + 1
224	                continue
225	            self.cards[cid] = c
226	        if dupes:
227	            n = sum(dupes.values()) - len(dupes)
228	            raise ValueError(
229	                f'{len(dupes)} DUPLICATE CARD ID(S) ({n} cards would be silently dropped) — a bundle '
230	                f'whose ids are ambiguous is not a bundle, and a sentence citing one of them names no '
231	                f'determinate evidence. Offenders: {sorted(dupes)[:3]}')
232	        self._cache: dict[str, Resolution] = {}
233	        # Every surname and venue the bundle knows. Used ONLY NEGATIVELY — to refuse a source name in
234	        # a lane that is not allowed to have one. We never infer WHICH source from a surname again.
235	        self._source_words: set[str] = set()
236	        for c in self.cards.values():
237	            for a in (c.get('authors') or []):
238	                if len(a) >= 4:
239	                    self._source_words.add(a.lower())
240	        for w in graph.works.values():
241	            for a in (w.authors or []):
242	                if len(a) >= 4:
243	                    self._source_words.add(a.lower())
244	
245	    # -- THE ONLY DOOR ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
246	    def resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
247	        """sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression
248	        -> attribution.
249	
250	        EVERY step is re-derived from the graph HERE, at use time. Nothing stored on the card is
251	        trusted: not the attribution, not the permitted set, not the expression, not the hash.
252	
253	        `verify_span()` is called FIRST — before any field of the card is read for any purpose — because
254	        a card whose bytes do not verify is not a weak card, it is NOT A CARD, and reading its `venue`
255	        to make a decision is how a landing page became a peer-reviewed article.
256	        """
257	        if card_id in self._cache:
258	            return self._cache[card_id]
259	        r = self._resolve(card_id)
260	        self._cache[card_id] = r
261	        return r
262	
263	    def _resolve(self, card_id: str) -> Resolution:
264	        c = self.cards.get(card_id)
265	        if c is None:
266	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='NO_SUCH_CARD_IN_BUNDLE')
267	
268	        binding = _binding_from_card(c)
269	        if not binding:
270	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal='CARD_IS_UNBOUND (no manifestation_id/content_hash '
271	                                                      '/span offsets — a DOI names a work, and a work has '
272	                                                      'no bytes)')
273	        # ---- 1. THE BYTES. Untrusted input; re-check offsets, hash, and the text itself.
274	        if not self.graph.verify_span(binding):
275	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SPAN_DOES_NOT_VERIFY against '
276	                                                      f'{binding["manifestation_id"]}')
277	        mid = binding['manifestation_id']
278	        m = self.graph.manifestations[mid]
279	
280	        # ---- 2. THE POLICY. What, if anything, may THIS SPAN name?
281	        #         Sol V9 §4: the question is asked about the BINDING, not about the manifestation. A
282	        #         manifestation-wide answer would hand THIS span whatever permission any OTHER span in
283	        #         the same document had earned — and the spans peer review rewrote sit in the very same
284	        #         document as the spans it left alone. Permission is per span or it is fiction.
285	        att = self.graph.resolve_attribution(binding, self.policy)
286	        if not att.admitted:
287	            return Resolution(False, card_id, refusal=f'SOURCE_POLICY_REFUSES: {att.refusal}')
288	
289	        # ---- 3. THE CARD'S OWN STORED TARGET MUST BE THE ONE THE GRAPH RESOLVES *NOW*.
290	        #         A stored target is a cache, and a cache that is not checked is how a stale permission
291	        #         keeps naming a journal it was never allowed to name.
292	        stored = c.get('attribution_target_expression_id')
293	        if stored and stored != att.names_expression_id:
294	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
295	                              refusal=f'STALE_ATTRIBUTION_TARGET: card says {stored!r}, the graph '
296	                                      f'resolves {att.names_expression_id!r} under `{self.policy.name}`')
297	
298	        # ---- 4. THE ATTRIBUTION IS RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH. The card's own string is never read.
299	        expr = self.graph.expressions[att.names_expression_id]
300	        work = self.graph.works[expr.work_id]
301	        prose = render_attribution(work, expr)
302	        if not prose:
303	            return Resolution(False, card_id,
304	                              refusal=f'EXPRESSION_KIND_IS_NOT_RENDERABLE_AS_A_CITATION: {expr.kind!r}')
305	
306	        return Resolution(
307	            True, card_id, manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=binding['content_hash'],
308	            expression_id=binding.get('expression_id') or m.expression_id,
309	            names_expression_id=att.names_expression_id,
310	            span=binding['text'], span_start=binding['span_start'], span_end=binding['span_end'],
311	            work_id=m.work_id, attribution=prose, card=c)
312	
313	    def admitted_ids(self) -> list[str]:
314	        return [cid for cid in self.cards if self.resolve(cid).ok]
315	
316	    def names_a_source(self, text: str) -> str:
317	        """The surname/venue check, used ONLY to REFUSE. Returns the offending token, or ''.
318	
319	        This is not `_cited_cards()` coming back in through a window. That function asked "WHICH source
320	        is this sentence about?" and answered with a guess, then gated the sentence against the guess.
321	        This one asks "does this text name ANY source at all?" and uses the answer only to say NO. There
322	        is no lane in which a surname selects a card.
323	        """
324	        low = text.lower()
325	        for w in self._source_words:
326	            if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(w)}\b', low):
327	                return w
328	        return ''
329	
330	
331	# =================================================================================================
332	# 2. ATTRIBUTION, RENDERED FROM THE GRAPH — NEVER WRITTEN OR COPIED BY A MODEL
333	# =================================================================================================
334	
335	#: The year is PROSE, never parenthetical: every one of the 281 "(YYYY)" parentheses in the reference
336	#: system is deleted by RACE's ArticleCleaner. `Expression.attribution` is the parenthetical display
337	#: form (`Acemoglu and Restrepo (2020), Journal of Political Economy`); it does not survive the cleaner,
338	#: so we re-render the SAME FACTS, from the SAME NODES, in the form that does.
339	_LEAD_FORMS = (
340	    'Writing in {venue_the} in {year}, {who} show that ',
341	    '{who}, writing in {venue_the} in {year}, report that ',
342	    'A {year} {venue} study by {who} finds that ',
343	    '{who} establish, in {venue_the} in {year}, that ',
344	    'The evidence {who} present in {venue_the} in {year} is that ',
345	)
346	_TRAIL_FORMS = (
347	    '{who}, writing in {venue_the} in {year}, report that ',
348	    'in {venue_the} in {year}, {who} show that ',
349	    '{who} find, in their {year} {venue} paper, that ',
350	)
351	
352	#: WHICH EXPRESSION KINDS MAY BE RENDERED AS A JOURNAL CITATION AT ALL.
353	#: A `working_paper` expression is NOT renderable here, ever, in any form. Under JOURNAL_ONLY it never
354	#: reaches this function (resolve_attribution refuses it first) — this is the SECOND lock on the same
355	#: door, and it exists because the first lock is the one that was found unlocked.
356	_JOURNAL_KINDS = ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version')
357	
358	
359	#: Venue names that take a definite article ("in the American Economic Review") as against those that do
360	#: not ("in PLOS ONE", "in Technovation", "in AI and Ethics"). Getting this wrong is not cosmetic: the
361	#: released artifact said "in the AI and Ethics in 2022", and a reviewer who writes that has not read
362	#: the journal. The rule is the head noun, not a hand-list of titles.
363	_TAKES_THE = re.compile(r'\b(journal|review|quarterly|proceedings|annals|bulletin|letters|transactions'
364	                        r'|magazine|gazette|record|reporter)\b', re.I)
365	
366	
367	def _venue_the(venue: str) -> str:
368	    """'the Journal of Political Economy', 'The Quarterly Journal of Economics', 'PLOS ONE'.
369	
370	    Never 'the The' (the venue already carries its article) and never 'the AI and Ethics'.
371	    """
372	    v = (venue or '').strip()
373	    if not v:
374	        return ''
375	    if v.lower().startswith('the '):
376	        return v
377	    return f'the {v}' if _TAKES_THE.search(v) else v
378	
379	
380	def _who(work: P.Work) -> str:
381	    a = [x for x in (work.authors or []) if x]
382	    if not a:
383	        return ''
384	    if len(a) == 1:
385	        return a[0]
386	    if len(a) == 2:
387	        return f'{a[0]} and {a[1]}'
388	    return f'{a[0]} and colleagues'
389	
390	
391	def render_attribution(work: P.Work, expr: P.Expression, lead: bool = True, form: int = 0) -> str:
392	    """The attribution clause for THIS expression. Returns '' if this expression may not be cited.
393	
394	    THE MODEL NEVER SEES THIS STRING BEFORE IT WRITES, AND NEVER TYPES IT. It writes a bare finding and
395	    a card id; the prose below is attached afterwards, from the work and the expression the policy
396	    chose. That is why "Writing in the Journal of Political Economy..." can no longer appear over bytes
397	    that came from NBER Working Paper 23285: the sentence's journal name is not something the model can
398	    produce.
399	    """
400	    if expr.kind not in _JOURNAL_KINDS:
401	        return ''
402	    who, venue = _who(work), (work.venue or '').strip()
403	    if not who or not venue or not work.year:
404	        return ''
405	    forms = _LEAD_FORMS if lead else _TRAIL_FORMS
406	    f = forms[form % len(forms)]
407	    return f.format(who=who, venue=venue, venue_the=_venue_the(venue), year=work.year)
408	
409	
410	# =================================================================================================
411	# 3. ENTAILMENT — AGAINST THE VERBATIM SPAN, AND NOTHING ELSE
412	# =================================================================================================
413	
414	_STOP = {'writing', 'article', 'journal', 'review', 'that', 'show', 'shows', 'find', 'finds', 'found',
415	         'report', 'reports', 'reported', 'demonstrate', 'demonstrates', 'evidence', 'study', 'their',
416	         'this', 'these', 'those', 'with', 'from', 'have', 'been', 'were', 'which', 'while', 'also',
417	         'than', 'when', 'more', 'most', 'such', 'they', 'them', 'about', 'paper', 'papers', 'result',
418	         'results', 'finding', 'findings', 'authors', 'author'}
419	
420	_NUM = re.compile(r'\d+(?:\.\d+)?')
421	
422	
423	def numbers_in(s: str) -> list[str]:
424	    return _NUM.findall(s or '')
425	
426	
427	def number_stands_alone(num: str, src: str) -> bool:
428	    """`"0.2" in "10.25"` IS TRUE. A substring test leaks a fabricated effect size through any source
429	    that happens to contain a longer number with the same digits in it — and we deliberately flood this
430	    prose with figures, which loads that hole with live rounds."""
431	    return bool(re.search(rf'(?<![\d.]){re.escape(num)}(?![\d])', src))
432	
433	
434	def entailed_by_span(text: str, span: str, work: P.Work | None = None,
435	                     min_overlap: float = 0.25) -> tuple[bool, str]:
436	    """Is THIS text supported by THIS verbatim span? The span is the only evidence there is.
437	
438	    `claim` — the model's restatement — is not a parameter of this function and never will be. The old
439	    gate read `src = f'{span} {claim}'`, and since the writer was handed only the claim, the chain was:
440	    the model writes the claim -> the writer writes from the claim -> the gate checks the writing
441	    against the claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
442	    """
443	    src = (span or '').lower()
444	    if not src:
445	        return False, 'NO_SPAN'
446	    year = str(work.year) if work and work.year else ''
447	
448	    for num in numbers_in(text):
449	        if len(num) < 2 or num == year:
450	            continue
451	        if not number_stands_alone(num, src):
452	            return False, f'NUMBER_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}'
453	
454	    words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (text or '').lower())} - _STOP
455	    if work:
456	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (work.venue or '').lower())}
457	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ' '.join(work.authors or []).lower())}
458	    if not words:
459	        return True, ''
460	    src_words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', src)}
461	    if len(words & src_words) / len(words) < min_overlap:
462	        return False, 'CONTENT_NOT_IN_SPAN'
463	    return True, ''
464	
465	
466	# =================================================================================================
467	# 4. VALIDATION — EVERY NODE, EVERY TIME, AT RENDER AND AGAIN AT PUBLISH
468	# =================================================================================================
469	
470	MARKER = re.compile(r'\[\d+\]')
471	PAREN_YEAR = re.compile(r'\((?:19|20)\d\d[a-z]?\)')
472	BANNED_META = re.compile(
473	    r'\b(this report|this review synthesi[sz]es|the pipeline|retrieved|the question above|'
474	    r'span-grounded|telemetry|our system|we retrieved|the corpus|evidence card)\b', re.I)
475	
476	
477	@dataclass
478	class Failure:
479	    node_index: int
480	    kind: str
481	    reason: str
482	    detail: str = ''
483	
484	    def __str__(self) -> str:
485	        d = f' :: {self.detail[:80]}' if self.detail else ''
486	        return f'[{self.node_index}] {self.kind}: {self.reason}{d}'
487	
488	
489	#: A sentence boundary: a terminator, whitespace, then a capital. Abbreviations ("et al.", "e.g.") and
490	#: initials are not boundaries — the old splitter amputated every attributed sentence at "et al." and
491	#: filled the report with stumps.
492	_ABBREV = re.compile(r'\b(et al|e\.g|i\.e|cf|vs|Dr|Prof|Mr|Mrs|Ms|St|Fig|No|pp|vol|ed|eds|approx|ca)\.$',
493	                     re.I)
494	_INITIAL = re.compile(r'\b[A-Z]\.$')
495	
496	
497	def split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
498	    out, buf = [], ''
499	    for chunk in re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])(\s+)', text or ''):
500	        buf += chunk
501	        if not chunk.strip():
502	            continue
503	        head = buf.strip()
504	        if _ABBREV.search(head) or _INITIAL.search(head):
505	            continue
506	        if re.search(r'[.!?]$', head):
507	            out.append(head)
508	            buf = ''
509	    if buf.strip():
510	        out.append(buf.strip())
511	    return [x for x in out if x]
512	
513	
514	def _common(text: str) -> str:
515	    """Refusals that apply in EVERY lane, in EVERY node, including the abstract."""
516	    if not (text or '').strip():
517	        return 'EMPTY'
518	    if MARKER.search(text):
519	        return 'CITATION_MARKER'          # deleted by the cleaner anyway; never write one
520	    if PAREN_YEAR.search(text):
521	        return 'PARENTHETICAL_YEAR'       # deleted by the cleaner; the year must be prose
522	    if BANNED_META.search(text):
523	        return 'META_COMMENTARY'
524	    # ---- ONE NODE, ONE SENTENCE. THE LAW IS PER-SENTENCE, SO THE TYPE MUST BE PER-SENTENCE.
525	    #
526	    # The publisher caught this on the first real release, and refused it. A node holding TWO sentences
527	    # gets ONE sentence_hash — the hash of the blob — so the sidecar has no receipt for either sentence
528	    # as it appears in the file, and `verify_release()` finds prose in the judged artifact that resolves
529	    # to NOTHING. The receipt has to be per-sentence because THE JUDGE READS SENTENCES.
530	    #
531	    # (The offender was my own hand-written Methods paragraph. The law caught its author again.)
532	    if len(split_sentences(text)) > 1:
533	        return 'NODE_HOLDS_MORE_THAN_ONE_SENTENCE'
534	    return ''
535	
536	
537	def validate_node(i: int, n: Node, b: CardBundle) -> list[Failure]:
538	    if isinstance(n, ParagraphBreak):
539	        return []
540	    if isinstance(n, Heading):
541	        return [] if (n.text or '').strip() else [Failure(i, 'Heading', 'EMPTY')]
542	
543	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- ATTRIBUTED
544	    if isinstance(n, Attributed):
545	        f: list[Failure] = []
546	        if not n.clauses:
547	            return [Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'NO_CLAUSES')]
548	        if n.connective and n.connective not in CONNECTIVES:
549	            f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'CONNECTIVE_NOT_IN_CLOSED_SET', n.connective))
550	        for cl in n.clauses:
551	            why = _common(cl.text)
552	            if why:
553	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', why, cl.text[:70]))
554	                continue
555	            # THE CHAIN. If it does not resolve, the sentence does not exist.
556	            r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
557	            if not r.ok:
558	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', r.refusal, cl.card_id))
559	                continue
560	            # THE MODEL MAY NOT NAME A SOURCE. It names a card_id; WE name the source.
561	            named = b.names_a_source(cl.text)
562	            if named:
563	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', 'SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT',
564	                                 f'{named!r} — attribution is rendered from the graph, not typed by '
565	                                 f'the model'))
566	                continue
567	            # ENTAILMENT, AGAINST THIS CLAUSE'S OWN CARD'S SPAN. This is the line that kills the
568	            # fabricated binding: the clause that says "task displacement" names Bresnahan's card,
569	            # and Bresnahan's span does not say it.
570	            work = b.graph.works.get(r.work_id)
571	            ok, why2 = entailed_by_span(cl.text, r.span, work)
572	            if not ok:
573	                f.append(Failure(i, 'Attributed', why2, f'{cl.card_id} :: {cl.text[:60]}'))
574	        return f
575	
576	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- OWNED
577	    if isinstance(n, Owned):
578	        why = _common(n.text)
579	        if why:
580	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', why, n.text[:70])]
581	        # 1. NAMES NO SOURCE.
582	        named = b.names_a_source(n.text)
583	        if named:
584	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE',
585	                            f'{named!r} — a sentence that names a source is ATTRIBUTED, and must be '
586	                            f'entailed by that source. It may not enter through the owned lane.')]
587	        # 2. CARRIES NO NEW PARTICULAR. A figure in the reviewer's own voice is sourced to nothing.
588	        if re.search(r'\d', n.text):
589	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER', n.text[:70])]
590	        # 3. If it is licensed by premises, it must be a TYPED SYNTHESIS over them.
591	        if n.premise_ids:
592	            prem: dict[str, Premise] = {}
593	            for pid in n.premise_ids:
594	                r = b.resolve(pid)
595	                if not r.ok:
596	                    return [Failure(i, 'Owned', f'PREMISE_UNRESOLVED: {r.refusal}', pid)]
597	                c = r.card
598	                prem[pid] = Premise(
599	                    id=pid, text=r.span, source=r.attribution,
600	                    level=c.get('level', '') or c.get('unit_of_analysis', ''),
601	                    horizon=c.get('horizon', ''), method=c.get('method', '') or c.get('design', ''),
602	                    mechanisms=c.get('mechanisms') or [])
603	            if len(prem) < 2:
604	                return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'SYNTHESIS_NEEDS_2_PREMISES', str(n.premise_ids))]
605	            for op in OPERATIONS:
606	                ok, _ = validate(Synthesis(op, list(prem), n.text), prem)
607	                if ok:
608	                    return []
609	            _, why2 = validate(Synthesis('CONTRASTS_LEVEL', list(prem), n.text), prem)
610	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', f'SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:{why2}', n.text[:70])]
611	        # 4. A FRAME sentence: licensed by nothing, so it asserts nothing particular. Already checked
612	        #    for numbers and source names above. That is the whole of what the law permits it.
613	        return []
614	
615	    # ---------------------------------------------------------------- TABLE
616	    if isinstance(n, EvidenceTable):
617	        f = []
618	        for cid in n.card_ids:
619	            r = b.resolve(cid)
620	            if not r.ok:
621	                f.append(Failure(i, 'EvidenceTable', r.refusal, cid))
622	                continue
623	            # EVERY FIGURE IN THE ROW MUST STAND AS ITS OWN NUMBER IN THAT ROW'S OWN SPAN.
624	            claim = (r.card.get('claim') or '')
625	            work = b.graph.works.get(r.work_id)
626	            ok, why = entailed_by_span(claim, r.span, work, min_overlap=0.34)
627	            if not ok:
628	                f.append(Failure(i, 'EvidenceTable', f'ROW_{why}', f'{cid} :: {claim[:50]}'))
629	        return f
630	
631	    return [Failure(i, type(n).__name__, 'UNKNOWN_NODE_TYPE')]
632	
633	
634	def validate_report(nodes: list[Node], b: CardBundle) -> list[Failure]:
635	    out: list[Failure] = []
636	    for i, n in enumerate(nodes):
637	        out += validate_node(i, n, b)
638	    return out
639	
640	
641	# =================================================================================================
642	# 5. RENDER — THE ONLY PLACE PROSE IS EVER PRODUCED
643	# =================================================================================================
644	
645	def _fmt_sentence(n: Attributed, b: CardBundle, form_seed: int) -> str:
646	    parts = []
647	    for j, cl in enumerate(n.clauses):
648	        r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
649	        expr = b.graph.expressions[r.names_expression_id]
650	        work = b.graph.works[expr.work_id]
651	        att = render_attribution(work, expr, lead=(j == 0), form=form_seed + j)
652	        body = cl.text.strip().rstrip('.')
653	        # lower-case the first letter of the finding: it follows "...show that".
654	        if body and body[0].isupper() and not body.startswith(tuple(w for w in ['AI', 'US', 'UK', 'EU'])):
655	            body = body[0].lower() + body[1:]
656	        parts.append((att + body) if j == 0 else f'{n.connective} {att}{body}')
657	    s = ', '.join(parts) if len(parts) > 1 else parts[0]
658	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s).strip() + '.'
659	
660	
661	def _table(n: EvidenceTable, b: CardBundle, limit: int = 14) -> str:
662	    import html
663	    rows = []
664	    for cid in n.card_ids[:limit]:
665	        r = b.resolve(cid)
666	        c = r.card
667	        expr = b.graph.expressions[r.names_expression_id]
668	        work = b.graph.works[expr.work_id]
669	        who = _who(work)
670	        claim = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', html.unescape(c.get('claim') or '')).rstrip('.')
671	        if len(claim) > 155:
672	            claim = claim[:152].rsplit(' ', 1)[0] + '...'
673	        cell = lambda x: html.unescape(str(x or '--')).replace('|', '/')
674	        rows.append(f"| {cell(who)}, {work.year} | {cell(work.venue)[:34]} | "
675	                    f"{cell(c.get('level') or c.get('unit_of_analysis'))} | "
676	                    f"{cell(c.get('method') or c.get('design'))} | {claim} |")
677	    if len(rows) < 3:
678	        return ''
679	    return '\n'.join(['', n.caption, '',
680	                      '| Study | Journal | Level | Method | Quantitative finding |',
681	                      '|---|---|---|---|---|'] + rows)
682	
683	
684	def sentence_hash(s: str) -> str:
685	    """The sidecar's key. Normalised, so a whitespace edit to the released file is still resolvable —
686	    and a WORD edit is not."""
687	    return hashlib.sha256(re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', s.strip()).encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
688	
689	
690	def render(nodes: list[Node], b: CardBundle) -> tuple[str, list[dict]]:
691	    """-> (markdown, sidecar). REFUSES to render an invalid report; it does not skip bad nodes.
692	
693	    Skipping is what a composer does. A composer that drops the sentences it cannot justify still
694	    produces a document, and the document it produces is the one that gets published. THIS returns
695	    nothing at all unless EVERY node is lawful — which is why `publisher.py` can trust what it is
696	    handed, and why it re-validates anyway.
697	    """
698	    fails = validate_report(nodes, b)
699	    if fails:
700	        raise ValueError(f'{len(fails)} unlawful node(s); NOTHING IS RENDERED:\n  - ' +
701	                         '\n  - '.join(str(f) for f in fails[:25]))
702	    md: list[str] = []
703	    sidecar: list[dict] = []
704	    para: list[str] = []
705	    form_seed = 0
706	
707	    def flush():
708	        if para:
709	            md.append(' '.join(para))
710	            md.append('')
711	            para.clear()
712	
713	    for n in nodes:
714	        if isinstance(n, ParagraphBreak):
715	            flush()
716	        elif isinstance(n, Heading):
717	            flush()
718	            md.append(f"{'#' * n.level} {n.text}")
719	            md.append('')
720	        elif isinstance(n, Attributed):
721	            s = _fmt_sentence(n, b, form_seed)
722	            form_seed += 1
723	            para.append(s)
724	            for cl in n.clauses:
725	                r = b.resolve(cl.card_id)
726	                sidecar.append(dict(
727	                    sentence_hash=sentence_hash(s), sentence=s, voice='ATTRIBUTED',
728	                    card_id=cl.card_id, manifestation_id=r.manifestation_id,
729	                    content_hash=r.content_hash, span_start=r.span_start, span_end=r.span_end,
730	                    span=r.span, work_id=r.work_id, expression_id=r.expression_id,
731	                    names_expression_id=r.names_expression_id, policy=b.policy.name,
732	                    attribution=r.attribution))
733	        elif isinstance(n, Owned):
734	            s = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', n.text.strip())
735	            para.append(s)
736	            sidecar.append(dict(sentence_hash=sentence_hash(s), sentence=s, voice='OWNED',
737	                                premise_ids=list(n.premise_ids), card_id=None, manifestation_id=None,
738	                                content_hash=None, policy=b.policy.name))
739	        elif isinstance(n, EvidenceTable):
740	            flush()
741	            t = _table(n, b)
742	            if t:
743	                md.append(t)
744	                md.append('')
745	                for cid in n.card_ids:
746	                    r = b.resolve(cid)
747	                    row = f'TABLE_ROW::{cid}'
748	                    sidecar.append(dict(sentence_hash=sentence_hash(row), sentence=row, voice='TABLE',
749	                                        card_id=cid, manifestation_id=r.manifestation_id,
750	                                        content_hash=r.content_hash, span_start=r.span_start,
751	                                        span_end=r.span_end, span=r.span, work_id=r.work_id,
752	                                        expression_id=r.expression_id,
753	                                        names_expression_id=r.names_expression_id,
754	                                        policy=b.policy.name, attribution=r.attribution))
755	    flush()
756	    out = re.sub(r'\n{3,}', '\n\n', '\n'.join(md)).strip() + '\n'
757	    # "Writing in the {venue}" + venue "The Quarterly Journal of Economics" -> "in the The Quarterly".
758	    # `_venue_the()` already prevents it at the source; this is belt-and-braces ON THE RENDERED BYTES,
759	    # because the last time this correction existed it ran AFTER write_text() on a dead variable and the
760	    # disk kept the broken string while the metrics described the fixed one.
761	    out = re.sub(r'\bthe The\b', 'The', out)
762	    return out, sidecar
763

content.text: Now let me understand how to build a real graph/bundle. Let me look at provenance, synthesis_contract, and an existing test that builds fixtures.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""TYPED WORK / EXPRESSION / MANIFESTATION GRAPH — attribution may name only what the bytes prove.
3	
4	WHY THIS EXISTS
5	
6	  The corpus was one row per DOI. One row means one identity, so the row for a QJE article and the
7	  bytes of an NBER working paper were THE SAME OBJECT. `wp_fetch.py` wrote the working paper's text
8	  into `row['fulltext']`; the row kept `attribution = "Writing in the Quarterly Journal of Economics
9	  in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane"`. The miner then bound spans to that row, the writer named the
10	  journal, and the gate checked the sentence against the working paper's span. Every component was
11	  honest. The DATA MODEL did the lying: it had nowhere to put the difference.
12	
13	  Sol, verbatim: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but
14	  they are DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven. The recovered text is presently A
15	  DISCOVERY LEAD, not automatically journal-attributable evidence."
16	
17	  So the row is split into three typed nodes, and the difference gets somewhere to live:
18	
19	      Work           the research object / case / trial          "what was studied"
20	        Expression   the version of the report of that work      "which version says it"
21	          Manifest.  the retrieved bytes + content hash          "the text we actually hold"
22	
23	  Spans bind to a MANIFESTATION, never to a Work. That is the whole point: `span -> manifestation`
24	  is a fact about bytes we hold; `span -> work` is a wish.
25	
26	THE CORE RULE (Sol, verbatim)
27	
28	  "Every span binds to its exact manifestation_id and content hash. ATTRIBUTION MAY NAME ONLY THAT
29	   MANIFESTATION, unless a stronger edge proves that the cited source contains the same span."
30	
31	  Implemented in `Graph.attribution_targets()`. An edge only widens what a span may name if it is
32	  SPAN-PRESERVING (exact_copy_of, authenticated accepted_manuscript_of) *and* ASSERTED. A
33	  `predecessor_of` edge never widens anything, however plausible: PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
34	
35	  "A title similarity match can PROPOSE predecessor_of; it CANNOT ASSERT exact_copy_of."
36	  -> every edge carries `status` (PROPOSED | ASSERTED) and a `basis` naming the evidence. An edge
37	     with no basis cannot be constructed.
38	
39	NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD (Sol, item 4)
40	
41	  "A universal word threshold cannot define 'full text': a short judicial opinion, statute section,
42	   trial-registry record, and journal article have different completeness profiles."
43	
44	  MIN_WORDS=2500 was my own invention and it is deleted. `profile()` derives, FROM THE BYTES:
45	  artifact_kind, sections present, body/chrome ratio, result-bearing sections, extractability,
46	  identity agreement, fetch outcome — and judges completeness AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR THAT KIND.
47	  A four-page judicial opinion is COMPLETE. A 535-word cookie banner is not, at any length.
48	
49	  There is exactly ONE reducer that may answer "is this the whole document?": `judge_completeness()`,
50	  driven by the KIND_PROFILE registry. `profile()` calls it, `Graph.from_json()` calls it, and
51	  `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` calls it. It was written twice before — 1,200 words here
52	  and FULLTEXT_MIN=2,500 there — which is not a shared rule, it is two rules that disagree, and the
53	  document a card cites depends on which module last looked at it.
54	
55	THE API. EXACTLY THREE ENTRY POINTS (Sol, "reality checks", item 5)
56	
57	    bind_span(manifestation_id, start, end)   -> bytes -> a bound span. BOUNDS ARE VALIDATED.
58	    resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy) -> what that span may NAME, under THIS task's rule
59	    verify_span(binding)                      -> the enforcement point re-checks EVERYTHING
60	
61	  bind_span() returns IDENTIFIERS — `expression_id` and `permitted_expression_ids`. It used to return
62	  `may_name`: a list of English SENTENCES. A sentence is a display cache, and a consumer that must
63	  string-match prose to decide what is legal has no rule at all — it has a suggestion. Prose is
64	  produced by resolve_attribution(), carried as `.text`, and NOTHING IS EVER VALIDATED AGAINST IT.
65	
66	WHAT RE-DERIVING THE LABELS FROM THE BYTES ACTUALLY FOUND
67	
68	  The memo assumed the six `fulltext_source='working_paper'` rows were working papers. The bytes say
69	  that label recorded WHICH FETCHER RAN, not WHAT THE ARTIFACT IS. It is false in both directions:
70	
71	    Parry et al. (2016), Group & Organization Management, "Rise of the Machines"
72	        -> the bytes are Yang-Hui He, "MATHEMATICS: THE RISE OF THE MACHINES", an arXiv PAPER ABOUT
73	           THEOREM-PROVING. None of Parry, Cohen or Bhattacharya appears anywhere in the 4,027 words.
74	           The title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines". This is not a predecessor,
75	           not a preprint, not a version — IT IS A DIFFERENT WORK, and 17 of its numbers were queued
76	           to be printed as findings of a management journal.
77	    Acemoglu and Restrepo (2019), JEP -> the bytes ARE the typeset JEP article. Not a working paper.
78	    Autor (2015), JEP                 -> the bytes are the AEA WEBSITE'S COOKIE BANNER. 535 words.
79	    Autor et al. (2003), QJE          -> readable body, but chars 0-19,569 are (cid:N) font codes and
80	                                         NOTHING in the bytes says whether this is QJE or NBER 8337.
81	                                         Genuinely UNRESOLVED -> therefore NOT journal-attributable.
82	
83	  And one the memo never suspected, found by the same content check, ALREADY SHIPPED IN A CARD:
84	
85	    Weiss (2008), Economics Letters   -> the PDF extracted as a CAESAR-SHIFTED GLYPH DUMP.
86	        span:  "...wkh qxpehu ri kdluguhvvhuv juhz pruh wkdq 633 shu fhqw ehwzhhq 4<<5 dqg 4<<<"

243	# 1b. PER-SPAN ATTRIBUTION — THE SpanCorrespondence  (Sol V9 §4)
244	# =================================================================================================
245	"""
246	THE API MOVED FROM MANIFESTATION-WIDE PERMISSION TO BINDING-SPECIFIC PERMISSION.
247	
248	The old shape was `resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, policy)`: ONE answer for a whole document. An
249	edge that widened it widened it for EVERY span in the manifestation at once — so proving that ONE
250	sentence of an accepted manuscript survived into the journal would have licensed the OTHER four hundred,
251	including the ones peer review rewrote. That is permission by association, and the association is exactly
252	what we cannot check.
253	
254	A `SpanCorrespondence` is the narrowest true thing: THIS span, in THESE bytes, is character-for-character
255	THAT span, in THOSE bytes. It carries both manifestation ids, both content hashes, both offset pairs,
256	both verbatim spans, the canonicalization ALGORITHM AND VERSION under which they were compared, the exact
257	canonical-span hash, and the identity decision for each manifestation. The verifier RECHECKS all of it —
258	the hashes, the offsets, and exact canonical text equality — because a correspondence that arrives from a
259	JSON file is an untrusted input, like everything else here.
260	
261	Consequences, all of them mechanical below:
262	
263	  * Repository metadata can NEVER prove span equivalence. There is no field on this record where a
264	    repository's opinion could be written; the record is made of hashes and offsets or it is not made.
265	  * A correspondence grants permission FOR THAT SPAN ONLY — not for every assertion in the manuscript.
266	  * "0.37 versus 0.2" FAILS IMMEDIATELY: canonicalization does not touch digits, so the canonical texts
267	    differ and `verify_correspondence()` returns False before any policy is consulted.
268	  * A span found independently in the VoR's own bytes should be REBOUND to the VoR manifestation
269	    (`Graph.rebind()`), which is strictly stronger than a correspondence: no cross-document permission
270	    is needed at all when the span is IN the document you want to name.
271	  * An accepted manuscript with no VoR bytes stays accepted-manuscript-attributable ONLY.
272	"""
273	
274	#: THE CANONICALIZATION. Named and VERSIONED, because two documents compared under different rules were
275	#: never compared. It does exactly two things — Unicode compatibility normalisation (so a ligature or a
276	#: non-breaking space in a PDF is not a "difference"), and whitespace collapse (so a column break is not
277	#: a difference either).
278	#:
279	#: WHAT IT DELIBERATELY DOES NOT DO, and why each omission is load-bearing:
280	#:   - it does not case-fold      : "Table 3" and "table 3" may genuinely be different objects
281	#:   - it does not strip punctuation: "0.37" vs "037" is not a rounding, it is a different number
282	#:   - it does not normalise digits or units: THIS IS THE ACEMOGLU TEST. 0.37pp in the NBER working
283	#:     paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE. Any "smart" numeric tolerance here would align them, and the
284	#:     whole point of this file is that PEER REVIEW CHANGES NUMBERS.
285	CANON_ALGORITHM = 'nfkc_whitespace_collapse'
286	CANON_VERSION = '1'
287	
288	
289	def canonicalize(s: str) -> str:
290	    """The ONE canonicalization. Its name and version travel with every correspondence that uses it."""
291	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', unicodedata.normalize('NFKC', s or '')).strip()
292	
293	
294	def canonical_hash(s: str) -> str:
295	    """sha256 of the canonical form, DOMAIN-SEPARATED BY ALGORITHM AND VERSION.
296	
297	    Without the prefix, a hash computed under algorithm v1 and a hash computed under a future v2 would be
298	    indistinguishable strings, and a stored hash would silently certify a comparison that was never made.
299	    """
300	    return hashlib.sha256(
301	        f'{CANON_ALGORITHM}:{CANON_VERSION}:{canonicalize(s)}'.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
302	
303	
304	def binding_id(manifestation_id: str, content_hash: str, start: int, end: int) -> str:
305	    """The identity of a BOUND SPAN — the unit `resolve_attribution()` now answers about.
306	
307	    It is derived, not minted: the same span of the same bytes always has the same id, and a binding whose
308	    offsets or whose source bytes were edited IS A DIFFERENT BINDING and cannot inherit the old one's
309	    permission.
310	    """
311	    h = hashlib.sha256(f'{manifestation_id}|{content_hash}|{start}|{end}'.encode()).hexdigest()
312	    return f'bind:{h[:16]}'
313	
314	
315	@dataclass
316	class SpanCorrespondence:
317	    """THIS span in THESE bytes IS THAT span in THOSE bytes. The only thing that can move a span across
318	    documents — and it moves exactly one span.
319	
320	    Every field is a hash, an offset, a verbatim string, or a decision derived from bytes. There is no
321	    field for a repository's version label, and that absence is the design.
322	    """
323	    id: str
324	    source_manifestation_id: str
325	    source_raw_hash: str          # sha256 of the source manifestation's text (its content_hash)
326	    source_start: int
327	    source_end: int
328	    source_span: str              # VERBATIM, as sliced
329	
330	    target_manifestation_id: str
331	    target_raw_hash: str
332	    target_start: int
333	    target_end: int
334	    target_span: str              # VERBATIM, as sliced
335	
336	    canonicalization: str         # ALGORITHM:VERSION under which the two were compared
337	    canonical_span_hash: str      # the exact hash of the shared canonical text
338	
339	    source_identity: str          # the identity decision for the SOURCE manifestation
340	    target_identity: str          # ...and for the TARGET. Both must be CONFIRMED.
341	    basis: str                    # how this correspondence was established. Never empty.
342	
343	
344	CORRESPONDENCE_IDENTITY_OK = ('CONFIRMED',)
345	
346	
347	def make_correspondence(g: 'Graph', source_mid: str, source_start: int, source_end: int,
348	                        target_mid: str, target_start: int, target_end: int,
349	                        basis: str) -> SpanCorrespondence:
350	    """Build a correspondence FROM THE BYTES. It is then still VERIFIED before it grants anything —
351	    the constructor is a convenience, never an authority.
352	    """
353	    src = g.manifestations[source_mid]
354	    tgt = g.manifestations[target_mid]
355	    s_span = src.text[source_start:source_end]
356	    t_span = tgt.text[target_start:target_end]
357	    cid = hashlib.sha256(
358	        f'{source_mid}|{source_start}|{source_end}|{target_mid}|{target_start}|{target_end}'
359	        .encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
360	    return SpanCorrespondence(
361	        id=f'corr:{cid}',
362	        source_manifestation_id=source_mid, source_raw_hash=src.content_hash,
363	        source_start=source_start, source_end=source_end, source_span=s_span,
364	        target_manifestation_id=target_mid, target_raw_hash=tgt.content_hash,
365	        target_start=target_start, target_end=target_end, target_span=t_span,
366	        canonicalization=f'{CANON_ALGORITHM}:{CANON_VERSION}',
367	        canonical_span_hash=canonical_hash(s_span),
368	        source_identity=(src.profile.get('identity') or {}).get('verdict', 'UNRESOLVED'),
369	        target_identity=(tgt.profile.get('identity') or {}).get('verdict', 'UNRESOLVED'),
370	        basis=basis)

479	#:     describing THE FILE IT DEPOSITED, and that is testimony about these bytes;
480	#:   * the cover sheet MAY NEVER ACQUIT — its DOI, its volume/issue, its citation block are the
481	#:     library CITING the article of record, which is precisely what a document that IS the article
482	#:     of record never needs to do.

495	def segment_cover_sheet(text: str, window: int = 12000) -> tuple[str, str]:
496	    """-> (cover_sheet_text, the_document_itself).
497	
498	    The boundary is the first form feed after the last cover-sheet marker — a cover sheet IS a
499	    separate PDF page, and that is the one structural thing reliably true of it. No marker => no cover
500	    sheet, and the document is the whole text (the common case, which must stay cheap).
501	    """
502	    head = text[:window]
503	    hits = list(_COVER_SHEET.finditer(head))
504	    if not hits:
505	        return '', text
506	    end = hits[-1].end()
507	    ff = text.find('\x0c', end)
508	    boundary = ff + 1 if 0 <= ff < window else end
509	    return text[:boundary], text[boundary:]

512	# THE REGISTRY — the ONE table that says what each artifact kind IS and when it is COMPLETE.
513	# =================================================================================================
514	# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
515	#
516	# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
517	# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
518	# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
519	# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
520	# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
521	#
522	# `expression` is the expression kind these bytes CONSTITUTE when they are the document — the link
523	# between "what is this artifact" and "what may a span in it name". A judicial opinion's bytes are its
524	# `official_text`; there is no journal version of it to widen to, ever.
525	#
526	# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
527	# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
528	# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
529	# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
530	# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
531	# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
532	
533	SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200     # scholarly kinds ONLY. It is not a fact about documents in general.
534	SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR = 120  # ...and below THIS, a scholarly fragment is a citation stub.

538	                                  expression='journal_version',     finding_bearing=True),
539	    'proceedings_paper':     dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
540	                                  expression='proceedings_version', finding_bearing=True),
541	    'working_paper':         dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
542	                                  expression='working_paper',       finding_bearing=True),
543	    'preprint':              dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
544	                                  expression='preprint',            finding_bearing=True),
545	    'accepted_manuscript':   dict(stub_floor=SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR, abstract_floor=SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR,
546	                                  expression='accepted_manuscript', finding_bearing=True),
547	    # A short official opinion IS the whole artifact. No results section, no methods, and COMPLETE.
548	    # stub_floor=None IS THE POINT OF THIS TABLE. Do not put a number here. Ever.
549	    'judicial_opinion':      dict(stub_floor=None, abstract_floor=None,
